sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Burnley are struggling, with no wins in their last 3 matches and a heavy 3-0 loss to Sunderland recently, though they showed resilience with draws against strong teams like Liverpool and Tottenham. West Ham United, however, have been more competitive, securing a win and a draw in their last 5 matches, including a notable 2-1 victory over Tottenham. Their attacking consistency, with goals in every recent game, gives them an edge.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two encounters, West Ham have the upper hand, winning 3-2 in November 2025 and drawing 2-2 in March 2024. Both matches saw goals from both sides, indicating a trend of high-scoring affairs. West Ham’s ability to score late has often been decisive against Burnley.

sections.subtitle: Team Statistics
sections.text: Burnley, sitting 19th with 15 points, have struggled defensively, conceding 47 goals this season (2nd worst in the league), while their attack has managed 25 goals. West Ham, 18th with 20 points, have also leaked goals (48 conceded), but their attack, led by in-form players like Crysencio Summerville (4 goals in last 5), has been more potent with 29 goals scored. Both teams struggle with clean sheets, suggesting an open game.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch
sections.text: For Burnley, Jaidon Anthony and Zian Flemming, both with 5 goals this season, will be crucial in breaking West Ham’s fragile defense. West Ham’s Jarrod Bowen (8 goals) and Callum Wilson (5 goals) are major threats, especially given Burnley’s defensive woes. Mateus Fernandes, with a recent high rating, could dictate play from midfield.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights
sections.text: Burnley’s low possession stats (41.1%, worst in the league) suggest they’ll rely on counter-attacks at Turf Moor, while West Ham may look to dominate the ball and exploit Burnley’s high concession rate (opponents create 76 big chances against them). West Ham’s tendency to score early (first to score in last 5 matches) could test Burnley’s shaky start to games.

sections.subtitle: Match Prediction
sections.text: Given West Ham’s slightly better form, stronger attack, and historical edge in head-to-heads, they are favored to win with Sofascore predicting a 45% chance of an away victory compared to Burnley’s 31%. Both teams’ defensive vulnerabilities point to a game with goals on both ends. Expect West Ham to edge it narrowly in a competitive clash.

halftimeSections.subtitle: First half overview
halftimeSections.text: The first half at Turf Moor saw West Ham United take a commanding lead over Burnley with a 2-0 scoreline. West Ham struck early with goals in the 13th and 26th minutes, capitalizing on their attacking opportunities. Burnley, despite showing some resilience, struggled to create clear-cut chances and failed to convert their efforts into goals. The away side demonstrated clinical finishing, which has put them in a strong position heading into the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance breakdown
halftimeSections.text: West Ham United dominated key moments in the first half, showing a higher expected goals value and creating significant chances. Their ability to win possession in the final third and convert big opportunities was evident in their two goals. Burnley, on the other hand, had a decent share of possession and managed several entries into the final third and penalty area, but lacked the final product. Their aerial dominance and duel-winning stats suggest they have the physicality to fight back, but they need to be more incisive in attack.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The momentum in the first half swung heavily in West Ham's favor during the opening minutes, with consistent attacking spells that led to their first goal. Burnley showed signs of recovery with a shift in momentum midway through the half, pushing forward with intent in several phases. However, West Ham regained control during a crucial spell that resulted in their second goal. Towards the end of the half, Burnley mounted sustained pressure with multiple attacking waves, but they couldn’t break through. West Ham closed the half with a late attacking push, underlining their threat on the counter.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players to watch
halftimeSections.text: For West Ham, Crysencio Summerville and Valentín Castellanos have been pivotal, with both finding the net in the first half. Their ability to exploit spaces and finish chances will be crucial in the second half. On Burnley’s side, players like Zian Flemming and Hannibal Mejbri have shown glimpses of creativity with shots on target and key passes. They will need to step up if Burnley are to mount a comeback.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Tactical outlook for second half
halftimeSections.text: Burnley are likely to come out with renewed urgency in the second half, pushing for an early goal to get back into the game. They may focus on utilizing their strength in aerial duels and increasing their intensity in the final third. West Ham, sitting comfortably with a two-goal cushion, might adopt a more defensive approach, looking to protect their lead while striking on the counter. Their ability to manage the game and exploit any gaps left by Burnley’s forward push will be key.
